summary: Market sentiment turned broadly bullish as the S&P 500 and Nasdaq closed at record highs, driven by a powerful tech rally led by strong earnings from Intel and an upgraded price target on Arm, alongside growing optimism that US-Iran diplomacy — with Witkoff and Kushner heading to Pakistan for talks and Iran signaling willingness to meet US demands — could ease geopolitical tensions. The dominant macro catalyst is the US-Iran conflict and ongoing nuclear/sanctions negotiations, with the US simultaneously tightening pressure through a global naval blockade and new sanctions on a Chinese teapot refinery buying Iranian oil, creating a complex push-pull dynamic between war risk and peace prospects. In equities, chipmakers Arm and Intel were standout performers, P&G delivered a solid quarter reinforcing its defensive appeal, while energy services firms SLB and Baker Hughes are benefiting from rising oil exploration spending triggered by Iran-related supply disruptions, and Spirit Airlines remains in distress as bondholders weigh a potential Trump-linked bailout. The primary risks investors are monitoring include a dramatic chokepoint disruption in the Strait of Hormuz — where only five ships passed in 24 hours — sustained oil price volatility, uncertainty around Federal Reserve independence, and the fragility of Iran peace talks that could quickly reverse if diplomacy collapses. In the near term, markets appear cautiously optimistic, with tech momentum providing upside support, but energy supply shocks and geopolitical escalation remain live threats capable of rapidly reversing the record-setting rally.
